authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 101 - Food Labeling
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for packaged-food statement of identity, ingredient declaration, nutrition labeling, and claims-related labeling requirements for FDA-regulated foods.

authoritative_sources.title: A Food Labeling Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's official labeling guide is the workflow reference for principal display panel, information panel, ingredient statement, formatting, and general plant-side review structure used during label-change and evidence-review work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: label-review packet assembly and operator terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Industry: Questions and Answers Regarding Food Allergen Labeling (Edition 5)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's current allergen-labeling guidance is authoritative for major food allergen declaration, ingredient naming, and current-thinking edge cases that often drive food-label escalations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: allergen declaration and formula-to-label consistency review

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for allergen controls, rework, corrective action, supply-chain programs, and recordkeeping that govern label-change execution and hold or release boundaries in human-food manufacturing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: change-control, hold, release, and corrective-action handling

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal source for traceability lot-code records and retrieval obligations that become critical when food-label changes, relabeling, withdrawals, or recalls affect covered foods.
used_for: record retrieval and mock recall posture

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's official FAQ set is authoritative workflow guidance for current food-traceability implementation posture, retrieval expectations, and the extended compliance date of July 20, 2028.

authoritative_sources.title: Fresh Foods Standards in Use
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US is a recognized vendor-neutral standards body for GTIN, batch-lot, and case-level traceability structures used across food-manufacturing and recall workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Labeling and Establishment Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture Food Safety and Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FSIS guidance is authoritative for establishment-side label responsibilities, approval boundaries, and record expectations for USDA-regulated meat, poultry, and egg-product labeling, which defines a critical jurisdiction handoff boundary for this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: FDA-versus-FSIS jurisdiction triage and approval-boundary handling

workflow_stages.name: request intake and jurisdiction scoping
workflow_stages.description: Identify product category, facility, jurisdiction, claim type, lot scope, and whether the request stays inside food-labeling process support before making any recommendation.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: obligation and label-control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map the product and package use case to the exact identity, ingredient, allergen, nutrition, lot-code, revision-control, and review obligations required before packaging, relabeling, or shipment can proceed.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: evidence assembly and consistency review
workflow_stages.description: Collect and compare formula records, ingredient statements, allergen matrices, nutrition support, artwork proofs, label specifications, supplier notices, and batch or lot records needed to support a bounded disposition.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: traceability and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible label revision history, lot-code mapping, hold status, supplier changes, shipment records, and relabel activity support traceable handling or require blocked escalation.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: exception, remediation, and inspection preparation
workflow_stages.description: Translate labeling gaps, jurisdiction conflicts, supplier-driven changes, traceability breaks, and plant practice drift into remediation and escalation packets without claiming final approval authority.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: finished-product specifications, formulations, and approved ingredient statements
allergen matrices, allergen-control records, nutrition calculation support, and claim-support context
primary and secondary labels, overlabels, artwork proofs, packaging specifications, and document-control revision history
production orders, batch records, lot genealogy, lot-code templates, inventory states, and shipment records
quality holds, nonconformance reports, corrective-action records, change-control packages, and release records
supplier and co-manufacturer change notices, approved-supplier records, specification updates, certificates, and packaging-material approvals
mock recall packets, traceability logs, consignee lists, and inspection or audit response notes
systems_of_record: document control or artwork management system

allowed: classify food-labeling requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
prepare evidence-backed control matrices, evidence-gap summaries, inspection-ready packets, and escalation notes tied to named artifacts and owners
map FDA, FSIS-boundary, allergen, nutrition, traceability, and release dependencies to review cadence and exception owners without improvising final authority
escalate: required tenant facts, product-category context, current approved artwork source, or source-of-truth mapping are missing and materially change the outcome
public-source, jurisdictional, or tenant-policy conflicts materially change whether a label, relabel, or shipment package can proceed
label-to-formula mismatch, quality hold, supplier-driven specification change, or traceability break increases release or compliance risk
requests for legal sufficiency, final label approval, claim approval, formula signoff, supplier approval, or regulator-facing response
refuse: requests for final legal signoff, guaranteed regulator acceptance, or final market-label approval
requests to proceed with packaging, relabeling, or shipment when required ingredient, allergen, nutrition, artwork, lot-code, or hold evidence is missing or contradictory
requests that collapse the lane into generic document review and ignore food-label-specific formula, allergen, traceability, or jurisdiction context

domain_failure_modes: Allowing packaging or shipment to proceed even though ingredient, allergen, nutrition, or artwork evidence is stale, missing, or contradictory.
Treating relabel or overlabel activity as a clerical fix without preserving lot-code, batch, and shipment traceability.
Applying FDA packaged-food assumptions to a USDA-regulated meat, poultry, or egg-product label without explicit jurisdiction confirmation.
Normalizing legacy label use after formula or supplier changes instead of escalating revision-control and quality-hold consequences.
Treating supplier-driven ingredient or allergen changes as label-only edits even though supplier approval, release, or quality disposition authority is still required.
Producing a confident food-label conclusion without named systems of record, current approved label revision, or accountable evidence owners.
Collapsing regulatory labeling into marketing claims, nutrition-science signoff, regulator negotiation, or final release authority that belongs to adjacent specialists.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FDA versus USDA-FSIS jurisdiction for meat, poultry, and egg-product labels
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack treats product-category and jurisdiction classification as a mandatory prerequisite and escalates to FSIS-aware owners rather than guessing when USDA oversight may apply.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Food Traceability Rule timing and applicability
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack records FDA's current implementation posture, including the extended compliance date of July 20, 2028, and still requires tenant confirmation of covered-food applicability before traceability-rule-specific conclusions.

notes: The public baseline supports food-labeling control handling, but tenant retrieval is still required for exact product category, jurisdiction, claim strategy, current approved artwork source, release owners, and rework or overlabel constraints.

The specialist is intentionally structured as operator-side regulatory labeling support and must not imply final legal signoff, final label approval, nutrition-science signoff, or quality-release authority.
